content.problemStatement:
Consider two **binary** arrays AAA and BBB, each of size NNN and a permutation‚Ä†^\dagger‚Ä† PPP of length NNN.

You will make two new arrays CCC and DDD, each of size NNN, such that:


Ci=APiC_i=A_{P_i}Ci‚Äč=APi‚Äč‚Äč  (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚Č§i‚Č§N)
Di=BPiD_i=B_{P_i}Di‚Äč=BPi‚Äč‚Äč  (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚Č§i‚Č§N)
Let us define:


The score of the permutation PPP as min‚Ā°(LNDS(C),LNDS(D))\min(\text{LNDS}(C),\text{LNDS}(D))min(LNDS(C),LNDS(D)), where LNDS(X)\text{LNDS}(X)LNDS(X) gives the length of **longest non-decreasing subsequence**‚Ä°^{\ddagger}‚Ä° of XXX.
F(A,B)F(A,B)F(A,B) as the **maximum** score over all possible N!N!N! permutations PPP.
Find ‚ąĎx‚ąąS‚ąĎy‚ąąSF(x,y)\sum_{x \in S} \sum_{y \in S}{F(x,y)}‚ąĎx‚ąąS‚Äč‚ąĎy‚ąąS‚ÄčF(x,y), where SSS contains all binary arrays of size N(‚ą£S‚ą£=2N)N(|S|=2^N)N(‚ą£S‚ą£=2N).

Since sum might be large, please print it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

‚Ä†^\dagger‚Ä† A permutation of length NNN consists of all integers from 111 to NNN exactly once. For example, [4,2,3,1][4,2,3,1][4,2,3,1] is a permutation, while [1,2,2][1,2,2][1,2,2] is not a permutation.

‚Ä°^{\ddagger}‚Ä° A sequence AAA is a subsequence of a sequence BBB if AAA can be obtained from BBB by deletion of several (possibly, zero or all) elements. For example, [3,1][3,1][3,1] is a subsequence of [3,2,1][3,2,1][3,2,1], [4,3,1][4,3,1][4,3,1] and [3,1][3,1][3,1], but not [1,3,3,7][1,3,3,7][1,3,3,7] and [3,10,4][3,10,4][3,10,4].


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, where NNN should be the length of the binary arrays.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the sum modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1032 \cdot 10^32‚čÖ103.
